QQQ.data_anomaly: QQQ price $713.13 is outside expected range ($250-$700). Verify data source — may be contract rollover, unit mismatch, or genuine historic move.

macro_summary: Friday's session saw the S&P 500 fall 0.78% to 741.84, with the Nasdaq-100 dropping 0.92% to 713.13. Oil surged 2.4% to $103.39 after fresh ship seizures and a drone-damaged tanker near the Strait of Hormuz, keeping supply fears elevated. The Trump-Xi summit concluded with no concrete trade deal, and chip stocks sold off on the disappointment, despite hopes of H200 sales clearance.
commodities_note: WTI closed at $101.16, down 1.18% from its intraday high but still up 6.02% for the week. Gold fell 1.27% to $4,561.20. The 10-year Treasury yield rose 3bp to 4.60%, and the VIX jumped 5.16% to 18.15.

accumulate_gates.note: All mechanical gates PASS, but momentum_warn is True (weak recent tape). ACCUMULATE requires either an explicit multi-year structural support cite (200-day SMA / prior cycle low / named floor) OR an imminent hard catalyst within 7 days (specific date). Generic fundamental praise does NOT clear the bar — default to WATCH if neither anchor applies.

singapore.summary: All three Singapore banks remain CAUTION on hard RSI and extension blocks. DBS +7% above SMA50 with RSI 81, OCBC +6.7% RSI 71, UOB RSI 66 — the cluster is uniformly overbought. NIM compression persists with US10Y at 4.60%, well above the 4.20% relief threshold. No entry possible until a pullback to 50-day averages or a dovish MAS decision in October 2026.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally bearish — dual NIM compression channels intact.
singapore.key_development: US10Y rose to 4.60%, worsening SG bank funding costs; all three banks now overbought with no near-term relief catalyst.

semiconductors.summary: Semis sold off Friday after the Trump-Xi summit yielded no trade deal and the surprise TSM share sale plan. SOXX fell 2.38% to 516.58, with all 11 members in CAUTION, most on extreme extension (>20% above SMA50). NVDA -3.7% ahead of its May 21 earnings; MU -5.2% on China disappointment. The cluster is still riding the AI capex supercycle, but technicals are stretched — no entry until pullbacks to SMA50 or named support zones. LITE's photonics deal and TSEM's $1.3B contracts provide fundamental support, but price entry points are far below current levels.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically extreme — awaiting pullbacks.
semiconductors.key_development: TSM announced a surprise stake sale, sending shares down 2.4%; NVDA H200 sales reportedly cleared but no deal signed, disappointing markets.

big_tech.summary: BigTech diverged: MSFT surged 4.24% on Ackman's stake disclosure and Azure AI momentum, becoming the only member not in CAUTION (HOLD). AMZN and GOOG remain CAUTION on extension (>10% above SMA50). PLTR currently CAUTION on high forward P/E despite below SMA50. The cluster's AI spending narrative is strong, but entry points are scarce — MSFT is now extended after the pop, and the others need meaningful pullbacks.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — MSFT tactically strong but extended, others blocked.
big_tech.key_development: Bill Ackman's Pershing Square disclosed a Microsoft stake (per NY Post, May 15), driving MSFT +4.24% and pushing it +6.6% above the SMA50.

interconnected.name: TSM (Taiwan Semiconductor)

interconnected.entry_note: Already on watchlist as CAUTION; becomes actionable if price pulls back to $367-370 (50-day SMA) after the share sale overhang clears.

interconnected.name: AVGO (Broadcom)
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC ramp (Google TPU, Meta) directly competes with NVDA's merchant GPUs; AVGO's earnings on June 4 will set semis sentiment.
interconnected.entry_note: Watch for post-ER pullback to $370-390 zone if AVGO guides above consensus; could offer entry into the custom silicon thesis.

active_risks: Strait of Hormuz blockade: WTI at $101.16, fresh ship seizures and a sunk cargo vessel raise supply fears; no ceasefire in sight.
US-China trade tensions: Trump-Xi summit yielded no concrete deal; H200 sales reportedly cleared but not finalized, leaving semis export controls unresolved.
Fed hawkish pivot under Warsh: April CPI 3.8% forces markets to price rate hikes; US10Y at 4.60%, straining Singapore bank NIMs and growth multiples.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil's sustained above $100 is elevating inflation expectations and complicating Fed policy, hitting bonds and extending semis caution. Singapore banks remain under pressure from high US rates and a strong SGD, with no relief expected before October MAS review. Tech stocks sold off on trade disappointment, but AI capex narratives offer structural support if pullbacks deepen.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Trump-Xi summit concluded with no tech deal, despite reports of H200 clearance; TSM announced a surprise stake sale, triggering a 2.4% drop.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: No new entries today; await pullbacks on all CAUTION names. Reassess after NVDA earnings (May 21) and watch for a VIX spike above 22.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive — 19 of 22 names are in CAUTION, mostly on extreme extension.
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: Nineteen of 22 names are hard-blocked on technical extension or RSI; only WRD (WATCH) and MSFT/CBRS (HOLD) offer near-term re-evaluation potential. The oil shock and trade disappointment keep entries scarce.

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: Guide above $80B, Blackwell Ultra ramp accelerating; semis broadly lift.
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Guidance disappoints or China restrictions deepen; semis sell off.

macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Hawkish hike or strong guidance on further tightening; rates surge.
